hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 binding by avoiding unfavorable interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid: strong SSTR2 binding with high selectivity over off-target SSTR1/3/4/5 is achievable by preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT7-10 and Cys3/Cys14) and disrupting off-target contacts at non-pharmacophore positions.

proposed_changes.rationale: Structural failures suggest excessive clashes in the binding pocket. Reducing the clash tolerance will enforce better geometry and improve structural quality.
The current pass rate is low (25%), and increasing the number of candidates will improve the chances of identifying more structurally valid and selective designs.
